report_name: Sygnia: Lone Actor Used AI as 'Force Multiplier' to Accelerate Cloud Attack

threat_actors.name: Unnamed AI-Assisted Actor
threat_actors.description: A single, financially motivated threat actor who used AI as a 'force multiplier' to compromise a global enterprise's cloud environment at high speed, executing hundreds of unique SQL queries and using multiple access keys in parallel.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts: Cloud Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The attacker used four different stolen cloud access keys for four separate accounts simultaneously from the same source IP, a behavior indicative of AI-driven automation.

attack_patterns.name: Network Service Discovery
attack_patterns.description: The attacker's AI-driven tools rapidly enumerated database schemas by executing several hundred unique SQL queries across dozens of databases simultaneously to identify valuable data.

attack_patterns.name: Account Discovery
attack_patterns.description: The AI-driven tools rapidly processed information from the compromised environment to discover new cloud accounts.

attack_patterns.name: Cloud Service Discovery
attack_patterns.description: The AI-driven tools rapidly processed information from the compromised environment to discover new cloud resources and databases.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Cloud Storage
attack_patterns.description: The attacker performed automated data collection by executing hundreds of unique SQL queries in parallel across cloud databases to identify and exfiltrate sensitive data.

attack_patterns.name: Automated Collection
attack_patterns.description: The attacker used AI-driven automation to perform collection at a scale and speed impossible for a human operator, such as executing hundreds of unique SQL queries simultaneously.

attack_patterns.name: Excessive Queries
attack_patterns.description: The attacker's AI executed several hundred unique SQL queries across dozens of databases simultaneously, a form of excessive querying to rapidly discover data.

attack_patterns.name: Obtain Capabilities: Artificial Intelligence
attack_patterns.description: The attacker obtained or developed AI-driven tools to use as a 'force multiplier', enabling high-speed, parallelized attack execution.

identities.name: Global Enterprise Victim
identities.description: A global enterprise whose cloud environment was compromised by a lone, AI-assisted threat actor.

infrastructure.name: AI-driven C2 Platform
infrastructure.description: A sophisticated command and control platform used by the attacker to orchestrate parallel, AI-driven actions across the victim's cloud environment from a single source IP.

indicators.name: Suspicious Cloud Database Schema Enumeration
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Cloud Database Schema Enumeration
id: 2b1c8e9f-4d5a-4b6c-8f9a-1b2c3d4e5f6a
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects SQL queries targeting 'information_schema', a behavior associated with the rapid, AI-driven data discovery phase of an attack.
  This technique was observed in an AI-accelerated cloud attack reported by Sygnia, where the threat actor used it to enumerate database schemas at machine speed.
references:
  - https://lasvegassun.com/news/2026/jul/08/sygnia-investigation-finds-ai-accelerated-attack-e/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-09
tags:
  - attack.t1046
  - attack.discovery
level: medium
logsource:
  category: database
detection:
  selection:
    Query|contains: 'information_schema'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects SQL queries targeting 'information_schema', a behavior associated with the rapid, AI-driven data discovery phase of the attack. The attacker used this technique to enumerate database schemas at machine speed.
